name: The wisdom of this world
notes: Across 1 Corinthians 1-3 Paul marshals a chain of Old Testament witnesses to overthrow human wisdom and ground all boasting in God. He cites Isaiah twice (29:14 against the wisdom of the wise, 64:4 on what God hath prepared) and once more on the unsearchable mind of the LORD (40:13), then seals the argument with Job 5:13 and Psalm 94:11 on God taking the wise in their craftiness and knowing the thoughts of man. Jeremiah 9:24 supplies the refrain that he who glorieth must glory in the Lord, which Paul quotes at 1 Corinthians 1:31 and again at 2 Corinthians 10:17. The shared core is that the wisdom and self-confidence of man are nothing before God, who alone is the proper object of all glory. Each citation keeps its own vantage: Isaiah and Jeremiah speak prophetically of the LORD, while Paul applies the same words to the gospel and to the Lord Jesus Christ.
